page: Microsoft Azure D96lds_v5 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the DLDSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated virtual CPUs, mapped from 48 physical Intel Xeon 8370C cores with hyper-threading enabled at 2 threads per core. The processor operates at a base clock speed of 2.8 GHz and includes 48 MB of L3 cache. This instance is configured with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled, providing 3686 GB of capacity, and the instance is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. No GPU hardware is included.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 93,258.02 and a Passmark CPU score of 76,019.64, both placing in the top 25% of tested hardware. Single-core performance is moderate, as evidenced by a stress-ng single-core score of 1,899.68 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1,711.0. Cryptographic capabilities are highly efficient, with the Passmark CPU encryption test reaching a top-tier score of 86,381.58, aided by hardware-accelerated instruction sets such as AES and VAES. Memory bandwidth is robust, registering 5,005.65 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 328.17 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads.

The primary architectural tradeoff of the D96lds_v5 is its low memory density relative to its high compute capacity. While the 96 vCPUs provide substantial parallel processing power, the 192.0 GB memory limit restricts its suitability for memory-intensive in-memory databases or massive datasets. However, this resource profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for compute-bound workloads that do not require large memory footprints, avoiding the premium associated with high-RAM instances. The bundled 3,686 GB local storage provides high-capacity local scratch space. This configuration is suitable for multi-threaded compilation, web serving, database operations, and batch processing.
description: The Microsoft Azure D96lds_v5 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 96 dedicated vCPUs (48 physical cores) running at 2.8 GHz. It features a low memory-to-core ratio, providing 192.0 GB of RAM (2.0 GB per vCPU), alongside 3686 GB of bundled local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the instance demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities, with a Passmark CPU score of 76,019.64 and a top-tier encryption score of 86,381.58. While the low memory density presents a tradeoff for memory-intensive applications, the high core count and substantial local storage make it a cost-efficient choice for parallel compute tasks, software compilation, database operations, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96lds_v5 is a compute-optimized server featuring 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB of RAM, and 3686 GB of local storage, delivering strong multi-core and cryptographic performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96lds_v5 is a compute-optimized server with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 3686 GB local storage for parallel workloads.
tagline: A compute-optimized x86 server featuring ninety-six dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, low memory density, and substantial bundled local storage.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C vCPUs operating at 2.8 GHz
192.0 GB of system memory providing a 2.0 GB per core resource ratio
3686 GB of bundled local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong multi-core performance and top-tier cryptographic execution
Suited for software compilation, web serving, and parallel compute tasks
